title: koeleria glauca subsp. maritima
intro: Koeleria glauca subsp. maritima, also known as sea finger grass, is a subspecies of the Koeleria glauca plant, a type of grass that is native to coastal areas. The medicinal properties of Koeleria glauca subsp. maritima include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support immune system health. Koeleria glauca subsp. maritima can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
